attrs.alt: Entra ID Audit Log flow and export destinations

content.text: Microsoft Entra ID Audit Logs record every change-of-state operation in the directory: user lifecycle events, group membership changes, application and service principal modifications, role assignments, Conditional Access policy updates, PIM activations, and device operations. Unlike Sign-In Logs — which track authentication attempts — Audit Logs answer the question 'who changed what, and when?'
Audit Logs retain events for 7 days on free/M365 tenants and 30 days on P1/P2 tenants. Stream to Log Analytics for custom retention up to 2 years.
Each audit event belongs to a category that groups related operations. The table below maps the most operationally significant categories to their common activities.
Add/delete/update user, reset password, enable/disable account
Add/remove member, create/delete group, update dynamic rule
Register app, add secret/cert, add owner, set API permissions
Add member to role, PIM activate/deactivate, create custom role
Create/update/delete CA policy, update auth methods policy
Create B2C user flow, update trust framework policy
Update company settings, update domain, update federation
Key Fields in Audit Log Events
The AuditLogs table in Log Analytics (and the Graph API response) exposes a consistent field set across all categories.
Actor — user UPN or app displayName + appId
Affected objects — UPN, objectId, type, modifiedProperties
Links multiple log entries for one logical operation
Which Entra service generated the event
Reading Audit Logs via the Graph API
The Microsoft Graph /auditLogs/directoryAudits endpoint exposes all audit events. Use $filter for targeted retrieval and $select to reduce payload size.

# List recent directory audit events
GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/auditLogs/directoryAudits
  ?$filter=activityDateTime ge 2024-06-01T00:00:00Z
  &$orderby=activityDateTime desc
  &$top=50

# Filter by category
GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/auditLogs/directoryAudits
  ?$filter=category eq 'UserManagement'
  &$select=activityDateTime,activityDisplayName,initiatedBy,targetResources,result

# PowerShell equivalent
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All'
Get-MgAuditLogDirectoryAudit -Filter "category eq 'UserManagement'" -Top 100
The Graph API requires the AuditLog.Read.All permission (application or delegated). Delegated callers must be in the Security Reader, Security Administrator, or Global Reader role.
Configuring Diagnostic Settings for Long-Term Retention
Diagnostic settings forward Entra logs to Log Analytics, Storage Account, or Event Hub in near real-time. Only one setting per destination type is supported per tenant.
Manage the diagnostic setting in source control so it survives tenant re-deployments.
Once audit logs reach Log Analytics, KQL provides flexible querying for daily operations and investigations.

// All audit events in last 7 days, summarised by category
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Count = count() by Category, OperationName
| order by Count desc

// Who created or deleted users in the last 30 days?
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName in ('Add user','Delete user')
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName ?? InitiatedBy.app.displayName)
| extend Target = tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName)
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, Actor, Target, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
Filter for Global Admin, Security Admin, and Privileged Role Admin assignments — the three most impactful roles in a tenant.

// Detect privileged role assignments
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has 'member to role'
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName ?? InitiatedBy.app.displayName)
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend AssignedUser = tostring(TargetResources[1].userPrincipalName)
| where RoleName has_any('Global Admin','Privileged Role Admin','Security Admin')
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, RoleName, AssignedUser, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
Secrets and certificates added to application registrations are a common initial access vector. Alert whenever credentials are added outside your change-management window.

// Application credential additions (OAuth secret/cert added)
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName in ('Update application - Certificates and secrets management',
                          'Add service principal credentials')
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName ?? InitiatedBy.app.displayName)
| extend AppName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend KeyType = tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties[0].newValue)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, AppName, KeyType, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
PIM activations grant temporary elevated access. Audit every activation to verify justification quality and detect off-hours or unexpected role use.

// PIM activation events — who activated which role and when
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Category == 'RoleManagement'
| where OperationName has 'Activate'
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend Role = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend Justification = tostring(AdditionalDetails[?(@.key == 'justification')].value)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Role, Justification, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc
A single actor creating or deleting more than 10 accounts within an hour is an anomaly — flag it for investigation whether from a compromised admin or a runaway automation pipeline.

// Bulk user operations — flag actors creating/deleting > 10 users in 1 hour
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where OperationName in ('Add user','Delete user')
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName ?? InitiatedBy.app.displayName)
| summarize Ops = count() by Actor, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where Ops > 10
| order by Ops desc
Sentinel Analytics Rule: First-Time Global Admin Assignment

The most impactful privilege escalation in Entra ID is adding a new Global Administrator. This scheduled rule fires only when an assignment is made to an account that was not a Global Admin in the prior 90-day baseline.

// Sentinel Scheduled Analytics Rule: First-time Global Admin assignment
// Trigger: actor assigns Global Admin for the first time (never seen in 90-day baseline)
let baseline_window = 90d;
let detection_window = 1d;
let known_admins = AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (ago(baseline_window) .. ago(detection_window))
| where OperationName has 'member to role'
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| where RoleName == 'Global Administrator'
| extend AssignedUser = tostring(TargetResources[1].userPrincipalName)
| summarize by AssignedUser;

AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(detection_window)
| where OperationName has 'member to role'
| extend RoleName = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName)
| extend AssignedUser = tostring(TargetResources[1].userPrincipalName)
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName ?? InitiatedBy.app.displayName)
| where RoleName == 'Global Administrator'
| where AssignedUser !in (known_admins)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, AssignedUser, RoleName, Result
Audit logs older than the portal retention window must be exported from Log Analytics or fetched via the Graph API while they are still within the 30-day API window.

# Paginate all audit events for last 30 days to CSV
$headers = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" }
$url = 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/auditLogs/directoryAudits?$top=999'
$results = @()

do {
    $resp = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $url -Headers $headers
    $results += $resp.value
    $url = $resp.'@odata.nextLink'
    Write-Host "Collected $($results.Count) events…"
} while ($url)

$results | Select-Object activityDateTime,category,activityDisplayName,result |
    Export-Csv audit-export.csv -NoTypeInformation
Write-Host "Exported $($results.Count) audit events to audit-export.csv"
Common Audit Failures and False Positives
Exclude SP object from alerting; monitor only human-initiated changes
PIM request denied by approver, or role already assigned
Check resultReason; no action if expected denial
App credential added by CI/CD SP
Exclude known pipeline SP; require short-lived federated credentials instead
Bulk user deletions near HR sync window
Employee offboarding batch from HR system
Correlate with HR feed; alert only on deletions outside sync schedule
'Update conditional access policy' by Entra Connect
Password hash sync or pass-through auth agent update
Exclude Sync service account; verify change content in modifiedProperties
Audit Logs record all directory change events; Sign-In Logs record all authentication attempts — both are required for complete identity visibility.
Free/M365 tenants retain 7 days; P1/P2 tenants retain 30 days. Stream to Log Analytics for extended retention and KQL analysis.
Key categories to monitor: UserManagement (account lifecycle), RoleManagement (privilege escalation), ApplicationManagement (OAuth credential abuse), PolicyManagement (CA control changes).
Diagnostic settings forward logs in near real-time; configure via portal, PowerShell, or Terraform IaC and store as code.
KQL queries in Log Analytics enable aggregation, role-assignment detection, app credential alerting, PIM tracking, and bulk-operation anomaly detection.
Build Sentinel scheduled analytics rules for the highest-impact events: first-time Global Admin assignment, credential addition to sensitive apps, and off-hours PIM activations.
Always exclude known-benign automation (HR sync SPs, CI/CD pipelines) from alerts to reduce false positive volume.
